records.ety: [OE. pretenden to lay claim to, F. prtendre, L. praetendere, praetentum, to stretch forward, pretend, simulate, assert; prae before + tendere to stretch. See Tend, v. t. ]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Pretended; p. pr. & vb. n. Pretending.]

defs: To lay a claim to; to allege a title to; to claim.
quotes: Chiefs shall be grudged the part which they pretend.

defs: To hold before, or put forward, as a cloak or disguise for something else; to exhibit as a veil for something hidden.
quotes: Lest that too heavenly form, pretended To hellish falsehood, snare them.

defs: To hold out, or represent, falsely; to put forward, or offer, as true or real (something untrue or unreal); to show hypocritically, or for the purpose of deceiving; to simulate; to feign; as, to pretend friendship.
quotes: This let him know, Lest, willfully transgressing, he pretend Surprisal.

defs: To intend; to design; to plot; to attempt.
quotes: Such as shall pretend Malicious practices against his state.

defs: To hold before one; to extend.
To put in, or make, a claim, truly or falsely; to allege a title; to lay claim to, or strive after, something; -- usually with to.
quotes: For to what fine he would anon pretend, That know I well.

defs: To hold out the appearance of being, possessing, or performing; to profess; to make believe; to feign; to sham; as, to pretend to be asleep.
